content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN. Initially, the array is filled with 000-s.

There are MMM types of operations that you can perform on array AAA. The ithi^{th}ith operation can be described by two integers (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã). In this operation, you choose a set of indices SSS such that


1‚â§j‚â§N1 \leq j \leq N1‚â§j‚â§N,
(j‚Äämod‚Ääyi)‚âÝ0(j \bmod y_i) \neq 0(jmodyi‚Äã)ÓÄÝ=0,
Aj=0A_j = 0Aj‚Äã=0,
, then you set Aj=xiA_j = x_iAj‚Äã=xi‚Äã for all j‚ààSj \in Sj‚ààS.

You can perform the operations in any order, but one type of operation can't be done more than once. What is the maximum sum of integers of the array AAA you obtain if you perform the MMM operations optimally?

For example, consider the array A=[0,0,0,0]A = [0, 0, 0, 0]A=[0,0,0,0].



Suppose x=3,y=2x = 3, y = 2x=3,y=2. Here you can choose indices 111 and 333 and set A1=A3=3A_1 = A_3 = 3A1‚Äã=A3‚Äã=3. So the array A becomes [3,0,3,0][3, 0, 3, 0][3,0,3,0]. In this operation you can't choose the indices 222 and 444 because (2‚Äämod‚Ää2)=0(2 \bmod 2) = 0(2mod2)=0, (4‚Äämod‚Ää2)=0(4 \bmod 2) = 0(4mod2)=0.




Suppose x=5,y=3x = 5, y = 3x=5,y=3 and you set A2=5A_2 = 5A2‚Äã=5. So the array AAA becomes [3,5,3,0][3, 5, 3, 0][3,5,3,0]. Here you can't choose index 111 because A1>0A_1 \gt 0A1‚Äã>0 and index 333 because (3‚Äämod‚Ää3)=0(3 \bmod 3) = 0(3mod3)=0 and A3>0A_3 \gt 0A3‚Äã>0. However, you could also set A4=5A_4 = 5A4‚Äã=5.




Suppose x=4,y=4x = 4, y = 4x=4,y=4. Now you can't choose any index because Aj>0A_j \gt 0Aj‚Äã>0  for all 1‚â§j‚â§31 \leq j \leq 31‚â§j‚â§3 and (4‚Äämod‚Ää4)=0(4 \bmod 4) = 0(4mod4)=0. So the array remains same.



**Note:** Since input-output is large, prefer using fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase contains M+1M + 1M+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,MN, MN,M.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers xi,yix_i, y_ixi‚Äã,yi‚Äã - parameters of the ithi^{th}ith operation.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the maximum sum of integers of the array AAA after MMM operations.
constraints: The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
